keyPoints: Internal commands are executed by the shell and are run inside the shell.
External commands are run by the shell and are typically found in the /bin, /sbin, /usr/bin, and /usr/sbin directories.
Internal commands can be modified or deleted using shell commands such as echo 'rm -rf internal_command' or echo 'rm -rf /bin/internal_command'
External commands are created using makefile or shell scripts and can be deleted using the rm command.
